file_insights.purpose: REST API controllers for Menu, Category, and Product modules
patterns: Comprehensive TypeScript typing with service response types

patterns_discovered.name: Hierarchical REST Resource Structure
patterns_discovered.description: Menus contain Categories, Categories contain Products, Products have Variants and Modifiers
patterns_discovered.evidence: Menu endpoints (/menus), Category endpoints (/menus/:id/categories), Product endpoints (/categories/:id/products)

patterns_discovered.name: Comprehensive CRUD with Advanced Operations
patterns_discovered.description: Standard CRUD operations extended with domain-specific actions like publish/unpublish, duplicate, reorder
patterns_discovered.evidence: MenuController implements list, get, create, update, delete, publish, unpublish, duplicate endpoints

patterns_discovered.name: Pagination and Filtering Support
patterns_discovered.description: List endpoints support pagination, filtering, and search parameters
patterns_discovered.evidence: ListMenusQuery, ListCategoriesQuery, ListProductsQuery parameters in endpoint signatures

patterns_discovered.name: Permission-Based Access Control
patterns_discovered.description: All endpoints decorated with @Authorized using specific permission strings

patterns_discovered.name: Multi-level Variant and Modifier System
patterns_discovered.description: Products support variants (size, type) and modifiers (add-ons, customizations)
patterns_discovered.evidence: ProductController endpoints for variants and modifiers with separate CRUD operations

patterns_discovered.name: Tenant-Based Multi-tenancy
patterns_discovered.description: All operations scoped to current organization via TenantOrganization context
patterns_discovered.evidence: @CurrentOrg() decorator usage in all endpoints, org.id passed to service methods

patterns_discovered.name: Audit Trail with User Context
patterns_discovered.description: Operations that modify state capture user information for auditing
patterns_discovered.evidence: { userId: user.id } metadata passed to service methods in create, update, publish operations

patterns_discovered.name: Rich Response Objects with Metadata
patterns_discovered.description: Services return structured responses with operation-specific metadata
patterns_discovered.evidence: CreateMenuResponse, UpdateMenuResponse, PublishMenuResponse types containing menu data and additional context

patterns_discovered.name: Relationship Loading
patterns_discovered.description: Endpoints support fetching related data (categories for menu, products for category)

patterns_discovered.name: Settings Management Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Separate settings endpoints using PATCH for partial updates
patterns_discovered.evidence: updateSettings endpoint using @Patch with MenuSettings merge behavior

gotchas_discovered.context: Single file contains three controller classes (Menu, Category, Product) which could impact maintainability and readability
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Consider splitting into menu.controller.ts, category.controller.ts, and product.controller.ts for better separation of concerns

gotchas_discovered.context: Permission strings ('menu.view', 'menu.create', etc.) are hardcoded in decorators with no centralized constants
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Create a permissions enum or constants file to prevent typos and enable easier refactoring

gotchas_discovered.context: @inject('MenuService') uses string literals rather than class references
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Consider using class-based injection (MenuService without string) if tsyringe supports it for better type safety

gotchas_discovered.context: Request DTOs (CreateMenuRequest, UpdateProductRequest, etc.) are referenced but not documented in this file
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Add validation rules documentation or link to DTO definitions for API consumers

gotchas_discovered.context: Only @HttpCode(201) used for create/duplicate, other endpoints rely on default 200
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Ensure consistent status codes across similar operations (PATCH should return 200 or 204)

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created a comprehensive menu controller with all required endpoints
approach_outcome.completeness: All three controllers (Menu, Category, Product) implemented with full CRUD and domain-specific operations
approach_outcome.code_organization: Well-structured with clear sections (List, Read, Create, Update, Publishing, Delete operations) and comprehensive JSDoc documentation
approach_outcome.conformance: Follows API_CONTRACTS.md specification with proper request/response types and permission models
approach_outcome.testing_readiness: High - clear endpoint contracts and type definitions enable easy unit/integration testing

recommendations.action: Split the 1670-line file into separate controller files (menu.controller.ts, category.controller.ts, product.controller.ts) for improved maintainability
Create a permissions.constants.ts file with all permission strings to prevent typos and enable centralized permission management
Add API documentation/OpenAPI integration to auto-generate API docs from controller decorators
Create comprehensive integration tests for each endpoint covering success cases, permission checks, and validation errors
Ensure all request DTOs have proper validation decorators and document validation rules in JSDoc comments
Add request/response examples in JSDoc for all endpoints to aid frontend integration
